title: New Testament Foundations — John
subtitle: The Gospel of the Incarnate Word
description: A structured study of John's Gospel tracing Jesus as the eternal Word made flesh, whose seven signs and I AM declarations reveal his divine identity and call for faith that leads to eternal life.
learning_outcomes: Identify John's seven signs and explain their theological function
Explain the I AM declarations and their connection to Exodus
Describe the structure of John's Gospel as Book of Signs and Book of Glory
Trace the light-darkness and belief-unbelief contrasts throughout the Gospel
Explain John's stated purpose in John 20:30-31

theological_framework.emphasis: Incarnation and belief as the path to eternal life

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

questions: What is the Prologue of John and what does it claim about the Word?
What are the seven signs in John's Gospel?
How many I AM declarations does Jesus make in John?
What is Jesus' prayer in John 17 about?
What does John state as his reason for writing in John 20:30-31?

next_course.title: New Testament Foundations — Acts
next_course.description: Study the Spirit-empowered advance of the gospel from Jerusalem to Rome
